chapter_title.en: Chapter 34 – Wergild for the injuries and the fractures

text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الذِّرَاعِ إِذَا ضُرِبَ فَانْكَسَرَ مِنْهُ الزَّنْدُ قَالَ فَقَالَ إِذَا يَبِسَتْ مِنْهُ الْكَفُّ فَشَلَّتْ أَصَابِعُ الْكَفِّ كُلُّهَا فَإِنَّ فِيهَا ثُلُثَيِ الدِّيَةِ دِيَةِ الْيَدِ قَالَ وَ إِنْ شَلَّتْ بَعْضُ الْأَصَابِعِ وَ بَقِيَ بَعْضٌ فَإِنَّ فِي كُلِّ إِصْبَعٍ شَلَّتْ ثُلُثَيْ دِيَتِهَا قَالَ وَ كَذَلِكَ الْحُكْمُ فِي السَّاقِ وَ الْقَدَمِ إِذَا شَلَّتْ أَصَابِعُ الْقَدَمِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, and Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, both together from Ibn Mahboub, from Ali Bin Raib, from Al Fuzayl Bin Yasaar who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the upper-arms when it is hit, so the wrist breaks from it. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If the palm is dried up from it, so the fingers of the palm are paralysed, all of them, so regarding it is two-thirds of the wergild of the hand. And if some of the fingers are paralysed, and some remain (healthy), so with regards to each of the paralysed finger is two-thirds of its wergild. And like that is the judgement regarding the leg, and the feet, when the toes of the feet are paralysed’.
